evals.prompt: I think this script is ready to publish. The hook is AI is changing everything and you need to know about this. The auditor gave it a 6.8.
evals.expected_output: REJECT: hook is generic (no specific number), 6.8 is below 7.0 threshold, with specific rewrite.
expectations: The response gives a REJECT verdict
The response identifies 6.8 as below the 7.0 minimum threshold
The response identifies the hook as generic (no specific number or fact)
The response provides a rewritten hook with a specific number or fact

evals.prompt: The pipeline ran once successfully in dry-run mode. Are we ready to go live?
evals.expected_output: NEEDS WORK: dry-run success is necessary but not sufficient; enumerate missing production readiness checklist items.
expectations: The response answers NEEDS WORK, not APPROVED
The response explains dry-run success alone is insufficient for production
The response lists specific missing items: social accounts, API keys, quality scores averaging 7.5+
The response requires 3+ real pipeline runs at target quality before go-live

evals.prompt: I wrote a proposal and I am pretty confident it is good. I am going to send it today.
evals.expected_output: Apply proposal reality check (client-specific win themes, claims evidence-backed, no empty adjectives, ROI quantified) — default to NEEDS WORK.
expectations: The response applies the proposal reality check criteria before approving
The response checks whether win themes are specific enough that swapping client names would break them
The response defaults to NEEDS WORK if any criterion is unconfirmed
The response requests specific evidence before approving the send
